content.text: Work the following problems and show all work. Support all statements to the best of your ability.

body.text: Is it true that every continuous map \(f:[0,1]\to[0,1)\) has a fixed point. Justify your answer.
Show that every compact Hausdorff space is regular.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: every continuous injective map \(f:(0,1)\to\mathbb{R}\) is an embedding?

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: every continuous injective map \(f:(0,1)\to\mathbb{R}^2\) is an embedding?

body.text: Show that Greek letter theta \(\Theta\) is not homeomorphic to the figure eight \(8\). Formally, \(\Theta=S^1\cup[-1,1]\times\{0\}\) and \(8=S_-^1\cup S_+^1\) are subspaces of \(\mathbb{R}^2\), where \(S_-^1\) is the unit circle centered at the point \((0,-1)\) and \(S_+^1\) is the unit circle centered at \((0,1)\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(A\subset\mathbb{R}^2\) be a countable set. Prove that \(\mathbb{R}^2\setminus A\) is path connected.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let \(B\subset\mathbb{R}^3\) be the union of a countable family of lines in \(\mathbb{R}^3\). Prove that \(\mathbb{R}^3\setminus B\) is path connected.

content.text: Answer the following with complete definitions or statements or proofs.

body.text: Let \(\mathbb{R}_\ell\) denote the reals with the lower limit topology. Is the subspace \(\mathbb{R}_\ell\cap[0,1]\)
Is every compact Hausdorff space normal?
Give definition of a locally connected space. Is every path connected space locally connected?
